title: Political executive will decide on Bengal law and order, says SC
content: SC was replying to an oral mentioning to continue deployment of central forces to deter post-poll violence in West Bengal

title: Kerala Election Results 2026 LIVE: Congress-led UDF surges ahead as Left's last bastion slips out of its grip
content: After the first few rounds of counting, UDF was leading in 100 of the 140 Assembly seats, while CPI(M)-led ruling LDF was ahead in just in 40 constituencies. The BJP-led NDA was leading in only one seat

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election results 2026 LIVE: TVK leads in over 100 seats as CM Stalin, other DMK veterans trail
content: TVK president Vijay leads in Tiruchi East and Perambur; Chief Minister M.K. Stalin is trailing in Kolathur as of 10.30 a.m.

title: Supreme Court notice on challenge to Transgender Amendment Act
content: A Bench headed by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant said the question of staying the Act did not arise as the law has not yet been notified

title: Kerala election results 2026: UDF leads in all three constituencies in Wayanad; Minister O.R. Kelu trails in Mananthavady
content: In the Mananthavady segment, CPI(M) candidate O.R. Kelu, a Minister in the present Cabinet, is trailing by 744 votes against his nearest rival, Usha Vijayan of the Congress.

title: Karnataka bypoll results: Congress party’s Umesh Meti leads in Bagalkot, BJP’s Srinivas Dasakariyappa in Davanagere South
content: Congress candidate Umesh Meti leads by around 10,534 votes in the Bagalkot bypoll against BJP’s Veeranna Charantimath; in Davangere South, BJP’s Srinivas Dasakariyappa in lead by 3,925 votes against Samarth Mallikarjun of Congress.

title: YSRCP slams Naidu over ₹20,439-per-sq.ft. spend on Secretariat, CMO buildings
content: Government spending a whopping ₹20,439 per sq.ft. for the Secretariat and CMO buildings in Amaravati, says Kakani Govardhan Reddy

title: West Bengal election results LIVE: BJP crosses halfway mark in leads; TMC leaders urge caution against early trends
content: Counting of votes for the high-stakes Assembly elections is under way, with the verdict set to decide whether the Mamata Banerjee-led TMC returns to power for a fourth consecutive term or the BJP scripts history by forming its first government in the State

title: Kerala election results 2026: UDF leads in all five constituencies in Idukki
content: In the third round of counting for the Idukki constituency, UDF candidate Roy K. Paulose has established a lead of 4,063 votes over LDF candidate and Water Resources Minister Roshy Augustine

title: Assam election results 2026: Major Opposition leaders trailing in early trends
content: Congress leader Saikia was trailing by 4,967 votes in Sivasagar district's Nazira seat. BJP’s Mayur Borgohain secured 11,607 votes after the second round, compared to Saikia’s 6,640

title: Kerala election results 2026: KPCC headquarters erupts in joy as UDF leads
content: As the counting entered the third round, the mood at the Congress headquarters turned jubilant, with party workers kickstarting celebrations by distributing sweets

title: Koridang bypoll results: BJP’s Daochier Imchen leads in Koridang Assembly bypoll
content: The bypoll, necessitated by the demise of sitting BJP MLA Imkong L. Imchen in November last year, witnessed a strong voter turnout of 82.21% on April 9

title: Operation Sindoor showed world Indian military's capabilities: Rajnath Singh
content: Addressing an event in Uttar Pradesh's Prayagraj, Rajnath Singh emphasised that while the armed forces "showed patience", they were clinical in destroying terrorist infrastructure

title: Space Wrap: From Sriharikota to Leh, preparations for Gaganyaan mission in full swing
content: This article is part of Space Wrap, a round-up of space news and developments in and related to India every month

title: Assam election results LIVE: NDA maintains lead in State; trends show party approaching majority mark
content: Both alliances are gung-ho about bagging at least 64 seats, the simple majority mark in the 126-member Assembly

title: Watch: What happens on counting day? | Explained
content:
Voting is over. Across Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Puducherry, West Bengal and Assam, the big question now is: who wins? 
With counting of votes set to begin on May 4, here’s a closer look at how the process is done in India — step by step.

title: West Bengal polls: Situation ‘fishy’, say cautious TMC leaders as several candidates trail
content: The trend triggered concern among TMC supporters, prompting party leaders to exercise caution in making claims of a landslide victory

title: Portion of bridge over Ganga collapses in Bihar's Bhagalpur, no casualties
content: As soon as officials received information about the gap between the two slabs, they acted promptly to remove all vehicles and people from the spot, says Bhagalpur District Magistrate Nawal Kishor Choudhary

title: ‘Kashmir will never be Pakistani and we will never become Pakistan’, says  Farooq Abdullah
content: “I pray that I will not die till I see the revival of Gandhi’s philosophy again”, says National Conference president

title: It’s a neck-and-neck race between Suvendu Adhikari and Mamata Banerjee in Bhabanipur
content: The second round of counting by ECI has indicated Banerjee leading from Bhabanipur seat by over 898 votes

title: Karnataka Information Commission has settled 40,000 second appeal cases in last one year, says commissioner
content: Commission held a special training workshop in Vijayapura to create awareness about the Right to Information Act for district-level officials and public information officers of various departments

title: Kerala Assembly elections 2026 results: Pinarayi Vijayan trails in Dharmadam after two rounds; UDF takes early lead
content: UDF candidate V.P. Abdul Rasheed surged ahead with a lead of 2,523 votes after nearly two hours of counting, overturning the LDF’s initial advantage in the constituency

title: Dharmanagar bypoll results: BJP leads by 13,988 votes in Tripura
content: After the third round of counting, Mr. Chakraborti polled 18,754 votes while his nearest rival, Mr. Bhattacharya of the Congress got 4,766 votes, according to the Election Commission website

title: FIR against former Chikkamagaluru DC, former RO, BJP candidate D.N. Jeevaraj on charge of tampering with ballot papers
content: The police registered the case based on a complaint by Congress candidate’s poll count agent soon after the BJP candidate was declared the winner following the recount of postal ballots

title: Silence, the court is in session
content: The resignation of Allahabad High Court judge Yashwant Varma last month, amid parliamentary proceedings to remove him over allegations that burnt wads of unaccounted cash were found at his Delhi residence in 2025, has brought into focus how the judiciary holds itself to account. Aaratrika Bhaumik reports on the opacity surrounding complaints against judges, the routine rejection of information requests, and the shrinking space for criticism

title: India-Nepal border: Crossing a line
content: With Nepalese law enforcement agencies pushing for customs duty on Indian goods above ₹100, people living along the Indo-Nepal border are alarmed over a disruption to their lives. A report on their worry over what will happen next at the so-far open border that divides the two nations, but is also symbolic of centuries-old relationships that has diminished that separation in practice

title: Leaders greet V-P Radhakrishnan on birthday
content: Born on May 4, 1957, at Tiruppur in Tamil Nadu, Mr. Radhakrishnan took charge as the vice president of India on September 12 last year.

title: BJP’s Harshad Parmar leads in Umreth assembly bypoll in Gujarat
content: BJP candidate Harshadbhai Parmar received 33,745 votes after the completion of the ninth round, compared to 23,310 votes polled for Mr. Chauhan, as per the Election Commission’s website

title: Pro-Pakistan slogans raised in Bareilly market in U.P., six detained; hunt on for other accused
content: Additional Superintendent of Police (City) Manush Pareek said police have been put on alert in the Cantonment area

title: Rupee falls 11 paise to 94.95 against U.S. dollar
content: At the interbank foreign exchange market, the rupee touched 94.95 against the U.S. dollar in initial trade, registering a fall of 11 paise over its previous close

title: Bowring hospital wall collapse prompts removal of dangerous structures across Bengaluru
content: M. Maheshwar Rao, chief commissioner, Greater Bengaluru Authority (GBA), has directed officials to immediately identify and remove dilapidated compound walls and buildings that pose a risk of collapse

title: Maharashtra bypoll results: Mahayuti candidates lead in Baramati, Rahuri
content: Sunetra Pawar, Akshay Kardile ahead as counting continues; margins widen, turnout crosses 55% in both seats

title: Overnight rain, thunderstorms bring temperatures down in Delhi
content: Rain lashed several areas in Delhi between late night and early morning hours. Safdarjung recorded around 5.7 mm of rain in the last 24 hours till 8.30 am on Monday, while Palam received about 5.7 mm.

title: LDF leads in 7 of 11 seats in Kannur as early trends emerge
content: In the traditional UDF bastion, Sajeev Joseph led by 2,393 votes in Irikkur, and Sunny Joseph maintained a lead of 111 votes in Peravoor

title: Assembly elections 2026 results: Counting of votes begins across five States
content: The results will decide the next governments in Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and West Bengal

title: Massive fire at scrap yard in Hyderabad’s Bholakpur triggers firefighting, late night evacuation
content: No human casualties have been reported. However, a dog domesticated within the warehouse died in the fire.

title: West Bengal Assembly elections 2026 results: No victory rallies will be allowed after announcement of results, says EC official
content: Special Electoral Roll Observer Subrata Gupta also said the poll panel is looking into complaints that agents of different parties were unable to reach counting centres

title: Kerala Assembly polls: Kerala all set for counting day as ruling Left Democratic Front eyes third term, while Opposition UDF hopes for a comeback
content: Kerala had 883 candidates in the fray on April 9 when, as per the latest updates, 79.7% of the 2.71 crore-strong electorate exercised their franchise

title: Pune rape case: Father of murdered child demands death penalty for accused; opposition slams govt
content: The real accused is this Government, which is useless and incompetent; you lathi-charged the people who were carrying that child’s dead body, Raut said

title: After hailstorm havoc, citizens of Malleswaram in Bengaluru pick up brooms to remove debris from streets
content: Despite civic workers working continuously, the city is yet to be cleaned of the trail of destruction from the rain on April 29

title: India-Jamaica ties characterised by ‘continuity and change’: Jaishankar
content: In an interview with The Jamaican Gleaner, Mr. Jaishankar also said that both countries “must continue to work together to build a deeper and more resilient partnership that works for both our people and contributes to a more just world”

title: High security in place to avert post-poll clashes in Kozhikode
content: The counting process is scheduled to begin at 8 a.m. at three centres— JDT Group of Institutions, Vellimadukunnu; Government College, Madappally; and St. Mary’s Higher Secondary School, Koodathayi— under tight police protection

title: Centre revamps Rashtriya Bal Swasthya Karyakram, adds mental health, NCD screening
content: As part of the government’s digital health push, RBSK 2.0 introduces digital health cards, real-time data systems and integrated platforms for monitoring and service delivery

title: Health Ministry issues guidelines on childhood diabetes care
content: New national framework provides for universal screening, district-level diagnosis and free lifelong care, including insulin and monitoring, under the public health system

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Security tightened in sensitive areas ahead of poll verdict
content: Votes for eight constituencies will be counted in UC College, Aluva, and MA College of Arts, Kothamangalam, both within the rural limits

title: Counting of votes to be held at three centres in Ernakulam
content: Strong rooms will be opened at 6:30 a.m. in the presence of candidates or their authorised representatives, and counting will begin at 8 a.m. Postal ballots will be taken up first, followed by votes recorded in electronic voting machines

title: PM Modi, External Affairs Minister on major diplomatic outreach in May
content: The travels follow the tough message to Indian envoys on building the country’s image at the Heads of Mission conference

title: Assembly elections 2026 updates: All set for counting, ECI promises transparency
content: The Chief Electoral Officer said 53,984 postal ballots had been issued to service voters, of which 20,028 had been received as of May 1.

title: West Bengal waits with bated breath for May 4 Assembly election results
content: Counting will be held across 77 counting venues spread across 23 districts of the State; the Election Commission has deployed 432 counting observers (COs) to ensure transparent and smooth counting of votes on April 4.

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Women make up over 80% of the additional voters
content: Of about 25.3 lakh more votes polled this time than in 2021, around 20.8 lakh came from women, the highest in the last four Assembly elections

title: All set for counting; ECI promises transparency
content: Heavy security as counting of votes polled for elections to Assemblies of Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, West Bengal and Puducherry and  bye-elections in seven Assembly constituencies of five States to be taken up

title: Electoral debuts in Tamil Nadu’s past Assembly elections tell a mixed story of breakthroughs, missed chances
content: With the high profile debut of actor-politician C. Joseph Vijay’s Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam in the latest polls, expectations are high about whether the party can breach the Dravidian duopoly or merely dent its margins

title: Assam election verdict: Congress hopes to stop BJP run
content: The BJP-led NDA is confident of a hat-trick of electoral wins, while the Congress-led alliance seeks to shed the Opposition tag

title: Vijay’s Velankanni visit cancelled; visual of supporters chanting ‘TVK’ inside Basilica goes viral
content: Addressing reporters at the party office in Karattur near Gobichettipalayam, Sengottaiyan said TVK party president Vijay is to emerge as a decisive leader and that the 2026 verdict will be historic

title: As many say software is dead, Amazon Web Services CEO calls it a 'huge business opportunity’
content: AWS CEO Matt Garman views the AI-driven 'SaaSpocalypse' as a major business opportunity, not a threat.  He highlighted the rise of agentic AI reshaping work and announced new AI-powered tools like the Quick desktop app, aiming to revolutionize personal productivity.  AWS is also deepening its OpenAI partnership, integrating advanced AI models into its cloud services to meet surging demand.

title: Vijay for beginners: The cinema star who could become Tamil Nadu's 'Thalapathy'
content: And today, one of the starkest outliers in the early trends is the performance of Vijay’s TVK. But who is Vijay, who, if trends hold, could become the new Thalapathy of Tamil Nadu politics?

title: GT IPL 2026 playoff scenarios: Gujarat Titans back in the mix with 3 straight wins
content: Gujarat Titans secured a thrilling four-wicket victory over Punjab Kings, marking their third consecutive win and reigniting playoff hopes. Sai Sudharsan's steady fifty and Washington Sundar's crucial late hitting guided them home. Despite the wins, a negative Net Run Rate and tough upcoming fixtures mean Gujarat must secure at least two more victories to guarantee qualification.

title: Will Rohit Sharma play today for Mumbai Indians vs LSG?
content: Mumbai Indians and Lucknow Super Giants, both struggling at the bottom of the IPL 2026 table, face off at Wankhede Stadium. With playoff hopes fading, both teams are desperate for a win. Mumbai's campaign has been hampered by inconsistent performances and the uncertain availability of Rohit Sharma due to injury.

title: IPL 2026: From Abhishek to Riyan Parag — full list of Code of Conduct violations
content: IPL 2026 is witnessing a surge in disciplinary actions alongside thrilling cricket. Eight players and staff have faced penalties, from fines to demerit points, for offenses like audible obscenities, mobile phone use in the dugout, and aggressive send-offs. Captains are also being fined for slow over-rates, highlighting a growing concern over player conduct.

title: ‘You are a magician’: SRK’s emotional tribute to Narine after historic milestone
content: Shah Rukh Khan celebrated Sunil Narine's historic 200th IPL wicket, a milestone achieved exclusively for the Kolkata Knight Riders. Narine, the first player to reach this mark for a single franchise, also became the leading overseas bowler in the 200-wicket club. His exceptional consistency and crucial role in KKR's success were highlighted.

title: Mamata Banerjee vs Suvendu Adhikari: All eyes on high-stakes battle in Bhowanipore
content: West Bengal's high-stakes political battle intensifies as Mamata Banerjee and Suvendu Adhikari face off in Bhabanipur.  While Banerjee has a strong history in the constituency, a significant number of Muslim voters are under scrutiny, potentially impacting the TMC's victory margin.

title: ‘Teenon me khelunga’: Babar Azam shuts down format debate after PSL win - Watch
content: Babar Azam captained Peshawar Zalmi to the PSL 2026 title, defeating Hyderabad Kingsmen by five wickets. Azam emphatically declared his commitment to playing all three formats, silencing T20 relevance debates. He was the tournament's leading scorer with 588 runs. Aaron Hardie shone in the final with a stellar all-round performance, securing the victory for Zalmi.

title: ‘I'm vegetarian, need to get my protein’: When Google CEO talked about his breakfast
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai revealed his morning habits. He wakes early, reads news, and enjoys a vegetarian breakfast. Pichai is particular about his tea, describing it as very English. He exercises later in the day. Pichai also shared his favorite Indian dishes from Bengaluru, Delhi, and Mumbai. He enjoys Dosa, Chola Bhatura, and Pav Bhaji respectively.

title: Rape-murder of 3-year-old: Kin seek death, protest shuts down Pune district
content: A brutal rape and murder of a three-year-old girl has sparked outrage in Pune's Bhor-Rajgad belt, leading to a shutdown. The victim's family refuses to meet politicians until the 65-year-old accused, a repeat offender, is sentenced to death. Protests erupted, with the Chief Minister assuring a fast-track trial and pursuit of the death penalty, while cautioning against public execution demands.

title: IPL: Sai Sudharsan scripts history, shatters Chris Gayle’s 13-year record
content: Gujarat Titans opener Sai Sudharsan has rewritten IPL history, surpassing Chris Gayle for the most runs after 50 matches with 2178 runs. He also recently became the fastest to 2000 runs in just 47 innings. Sudharsan's consistent performances, including a century and three fifties in his last four innings, highlight his remarkable rise.

title: Tensions intensify at Strait: Cargo ship sailing near Hormuz attacked by Iran
content: A bulk carrier was attacked by small craft near Iran's coast in the Strait of Hormuz, with all crew reported safe and no environmental impact. This incident occurs amidst heightened tensions as the US enforces a counter-blockade on Iranian ports and plans to guide ships through the strait, drawing a strong warning from Tehran.

title: 8 dead, several injured after car rams into group aiding accident victims in Uttar Pradesh
content: A speeding car tragically killed eight people and injured several others on the Akbarpur–Jalalpur road. The car rammed into a group of rescuers who had gathered to help victims of an earlier motorcycle collision. Police are investigating the incident and attempting to identify the car's driver.

title: Shivang Kumar: How father transformed the swashbuckling opener into a chinaman in a year!
content: Shivang Kumar made his name in the Madhya Pradesh T20 League as an opener. His exploits with the bat helped him become a marquee player for Bundelkhand Bulls, who picked him for Rs 13 lakh for the 2026 season. But within a year, he learnt the art of wrist-spin from his father, a former Bengal U-19 cricketer, and made instant impact. The IPL franchises noticed and multiple trials later, he was roped in by the Sunrisers Hyderabad.

title: Middle East crisis: Iran proposes 30-day plan to end war, says it has received US reply
content: Iran has received a US response to its 30-day peace proposal, conveyed via Pakistan. The offer aims to end the ongoing war, not just extend a ceasefire, and suggests delaying nuclear talks until after hostilities cease. The US President expressed doubt about the proposal's success, citing Iran's past actions.

title: More Strait talks: Trump rolls out 'Project Freedom' to guide ships through Hormuz
content: US President Donald Trump launched 'Project Freedom' to aid commercial ships stranded in the Strait of Hormuz, citing humanitarian concerns and low supplies.  He stated the US will guide vessels safely out, emphasizing these ships belong to nations uninvolved in regional disputes. Trump also revealed positive discussions with Iran, though he rejected their latest proposal for conflict resolution.

title: PSL final: Mohsin Naqvi hands trophy as Babar Azam lifts first T20 title as captain
content: Peshawar Zalmi secured their second PSL title, their first since 2017, by defeating Hyderabad Kingsmen in the final. Captain Babar Azam finally lifted a trophy as leader, while Aaron Hardie starred with both bat and ball, scoring an unbeaten 56 and taking 4 wickets to guide Zalmi to victory.

title: Will Mumbai Indians allow Jasprit Bumrah the rest he badly needs?
content: Jasprit Bumrah is shouldering a heavy workload for Mumbai Indians in the IPL, struggling to deliver wickets while bowling defensively and attacking. Despite MI's dismal performance and Bumrah's fatigue, coach Mahela Jayawardene is reluctant to rest him, citing mathematical possibilities. This approach echoes past decisions that led to Bumrah's injury and significant absences for India.

title: Today’s poll results may give a broad mood of nation; how it may impact equations nationally
content: Assembly poll results in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry will offer a glimpse into the national political mood. The outcomes could significantly impact the opposition INDIA bloc, with key contests in Bengal and Tamil Nadu holding particular importance for regional giants DMK and TMC.

title: Are Huma Qureshi and Rachit Singh set to get married in October this year? - Report
content: Huma Qureshi and Rachit Singh are reportedly planning to tie the knot in October or November of 2026. Following a private engagement in the US, the couple is said to be organizing an intimate wedding ceremony, likely in Mumbai, with a grand reception for industry friends to follow. Their relationship gained attention after they were seen together at various events. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Abandoned in forest, 2-year-old walks for 20 hours, survives
content: A two-year-old boy miraculously survived nearly 20 hours alone in a wild animal-infested forest after his father allegedly murdered his mother and abandoned him. Found weak and dehydrated by police, the toddler's incredible resilience in the face of extreme danger has stunned authorities. The father has been arrested, and the child is recovering.

title: Air India CEO race narrows to 4 names
content: The race for Air India's CEO position has narrowed to four candidates, including two Indian executives, Nipun Aggarwal and Vinod Kannan, and two expatriates. The airline's board is set to meet soon to make a decision amidst significant financial losses and operational challenges. The delay in appointing a successor to Campbell Wilson is reportedly adding to the airline's ongoing struggles.

title: Heart donated in Panchkula gives Sudanese teen in Delhi new life
content: A 14-year-old Sudanese boy received a life-saving heart transplant in Delhi, thanks to a donor heart flown from Haryana. The organ came from a 41-year-old woman declared brain dead, whose family bravely consented to donation.

title: From red to green to saffron? BJP eyes power shift in Bengal as Mamata defends her turf
content: West Bengal's political landscape, historically dominated by single parties for extended periods, is now witnessing an intense battle for the 2026 assembly elections. Chief minister Mamata Banerjee's 15-year rule faces a formidable challenge from the aggressively expanding BJP, which aims to end the TMC's reign. This election marks a critical juncture for the state's political legacy.

title: SG Tushar Mehta takes swipe at ‘bully’ judges in his book
content: Senior advocate Tushar Mehta's upcoming book criticizes some judges for exhibiting 'judicial bullying' due to excessive courtroom reverence. He argues that while judges face immense pressure, this cannot justify incivility, as the public expects high standards from an institution they cannot easily remove. Mehta highlights the power imbalance lawyers face when confronting arrogant judges.

title: Team Chadha to get audience with Murmu ahead of Mann
content: Punjab's political landscape is heating up as rival factions, led by AAP MP Raghav Chadha and CM Bhagwant Mann, are set to meet the President. The dispute centers on alleged defections and police actions, with Chadha planning to highlight a "political vendetta" following FIRs against fellow MP Sandeep Pathak. BJP has also voiced concerns over alleged retaliation.

title: Assembly elections highlights: Trends show BJP ahead in Bengal, Assam; close contest in Tamil Nadu, Kerala
content: Seats 265 / 294 Seats 227 / 234 Seats 122 / 140 Seats 114 / 126 Seats 22 / 30 The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. title: Iran War, Oil Crisis Has 'Enormous Impact' In Asia-pacific: Japan PM
content: Roughly one-fifth of the world's oil normally flows through the Strait of Hormuz, where shipping has been throttled by Iran since its war with the US.

content: Ambedkar Nagar SP Prachi Singh said the encounter took place on the intervening night of Sunday-Monday.

title: No Hearse, Stretcher, Madhya Pradesh Family Carries Teen's Body On Shoulders
content: A 15-year-old tribal girl’s body was left on a roadside for hours after post-mortem in Madhya Pradesh due to lack of official support.

content: Puducherry Assembly Election Results 2026 LIVE Updates: A record voter turnout of 89.87% was reported, marking the highest participation ever in the Union Territory since its merger with India in 1964.

title: Actor Vijay And His Search For A Big Political Role In Tamil Nadu
content: For Vijay, the electoral fate of cinema icons Sivaji Ganesan, Kamal Hassan, Vijaykanth, and Khushbu would serve as a cautionary tale.

title: Overnight Rain, Thunderstorms In Delhi Bring Relief From Heat
content: Palam recorded a low of 17.9 degrees Celsius, 7.7 degrees below normal, while The Ridge logged a minimum temperature of 18 degrees Celsius, down by 7 degrees.

title: Video: United Airlines Flight Hits Truck While Landing At US Airport
content: No injuries were reported on the plane, and the truck driver was taken to the hospital with minor injuries and released, the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey said in a statement

title: United Airlines Jet Clips Lamppost And Truck Near Newark Airport
content: While a United Airlines Aircraft was approaching the runway, a landing tire and the underside of the plane collided with a pole and a tractor-trailer.

title: Markets News LIVE: Sensex Up By 600 Points, Nifty Tops 2400 As Envestors Eye State Poll Results

title: Suvendu Adhikari, The Student Who One-Upped The Master, And Is Trying Again
content: Adhikari joined Banerjee's Trinamool Congress in 1998 and entered the Bengal assembly as an MLA from the Kanthi Dakshin constituency in 2006.

content: Although the outcome of these bypolls is unlikely to have an immediate impact on state politics, the contest is seen as a matter of prestige for both the ruling Congress and the opposition BJP.
